entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 18 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-18 03:48AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • AFL 2022 round 14 LIVE updates: Power battle to stay in finals race against red-hot Swans
  • Diplomats danced to ‘I Will Survive’ while closing historic Geneva trade deal
  • Monkey shot, tiger roams streets as toll mounts for cartels’ exotic pets
  • ‘A simple protest that got out of hand’: Trump defends role in Capitol attack
  • How do you get a job after prison? Ask former inmate, Luke, who has beaten the odds
  • Kyrgios pulls out an astonishing 'tweener'
  • Harten soul going into Giants’ push for first championship
  • Greens dump new convenor for trans comments
  • How Bluey went from a kids’ TV show to a global merchandising machine
  • Collingwood looking into Jordan De Goey Bali party video
  • Deborah James shares emotional health update
  • How to tap your home equity for an income stream
  • England smash 498 against the Netherlands to break ODI record
  • Smith, Mickelson miss the cut at US Open
  • Eurovision rejects Ukraine call to host contest in Kyiv, talks to BBC
  • ‘He won’t be considered’: No SOS call to Latrell after Wighton COVID-19 positive
  • ‘Unusually undesigned’: Troye Sivan’s home shows livability is in, gimmicky is out
  • Melbourne company constructing entire homes by 3D printing
  • Best of cartoons, June 18, 2022
  • Mask mandate in Victorian airports lifts, workplace jab rules changed
  • How to rouse a sleeping giant? The burning question for energy-rich Australia
  • Labor and the curse of the global shocks: Can Albanese defy history?
  • The latest illustrations from artist Michael Leunig
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Davidson
  • Best of cartoons, June 17, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • Blackout warnings lifted for weekend as national cabinet weighs up energy solutions
  • Pauline Hanson’s Senate re-election confirmed after weeks of counting
  • $760m extra for hospitals while health system fixes are found
  • Uber calls for industry-wide earnings safety net for gig workers
  • Boats are not the problem, it’s the 130,000 asylum seekers living here
  • Eight of Sydney’s under-appreciated up-and-coming suburbs
  • We’re paying the price for privatised power
  • The energy crisis NSW didn’t need to endure
  • Detective took everything Guy Sebastian told him as ‘gospel’, court told
  • Corporate culture, not ‘bad apples’ to blame at Star, inquiry told
  • Detective denies ‘deliberate omissions’ in Dawson investigation, trial told
  • COVID-19 shots for babies and toddlers approved by US medical regulator
  • Discarded rubbish found on Mars
  • British government orders extradition of Julian Assange
  • Human remains found in Amazon belong to British journalist, exam confirms
  • Russian economy to overcome ‘reckless’ sanctions, Putin says
  • As Russia presses assault, Ukraine given possible path to join EU
  • UK orders extradition of Julian Assange to United States
  • Sogavare promises Wong no persistent Chinese ‘military presence’ in Solomons
  • New Zealand ditches COVID tests for inbound travellers
  • Ties off: traditional dress code ditched at Royal Ascot to cope with heatwave
  • Gina Rinehart, sanctioned by Putin, calls on Australia to ramp up defences
  • Beauty brand Revlon filed for bankruptcy
  • In Saudi LGBTQ crackdown, even rainbow toys aren’t safe
  • D’oh! David Littleproud’s nuclear comments insult our intelligence
  • Cost of strawberries grows higher than lettuce
  • Power supply issues restored on Australia’s east coast
  • ‘Perfect storm’: Is the Australian economy heading back to the 1970s?
  • ‘Restaurant owners swore at me’: Sally Capp on our re-emerging city
  • Artist Atong Atem: ‘Black women saved my life, I would say’
  • ‘Chrissy walks in with John Legend…’: Why LA’s in love with a former Aussie farm boy
  • ‘Left-wing weapon’: Opposition MPs speak out against Victoria’s treaty plan
  • Our fast guide to the energy crisis and what you can do about it
  • Gay, straight or whoever we are, our secrets need not be fodder for public curiosity
  • Game-changing reform promises a better future for our children
  • If we fail to prosecute war crimes, the law is a ‘dead letter,’ says inquiry judge
  • Good Weekend Superquiz and Saturday Target Time, June 18
  • SpaceX reportedly fired employees behind open letter rebuking Elon Musk
  • How Hong Kong roots fire UniSuper’s new CEO
  • Dither at your own risk, you may miss out on that promotion
  • ‘It’s ridiculous’: Architect of WA policy calls for national gas reservation
  • ‘Things can go wrong’: Judo Bank tightens lending as rates ramp up
  • Why Albanese needs to protect capitalism from the capitalists
  • Renewables help landlords dodge energy storm
  • Tenants see new chapters in older buildings
  • ‘BNPL crunch’: Humm, Latitude walk away from sale
  • ‘This is an airport issue’: Alan Joyce defends Qantas amid fresh wave of anger
  • ‘Run by dinosaurs’: the breath of fresh air women are injecting into Australian music
  • ‘It shocks me’: Melissa Leong on Australian TV and her chance at making history
  • Stars will align for one night only as Logies red carpet is rolled out
  • An octopus cracks a cold case? Mystery and a magical fantasy books to read next
  • Stories of lost souls, reality TV and anonymous sex make for vivid reading
  • ‘I was just unemployed’: The unknown Australian filmmaker who is suddenly everywhere
  • Watch the trailer for Children's Hospital
  • Photorealistic AI images have arrived. Are artists in trouble?
  • National weather forecast for Saturday June 18
  • I measured gas pollution in my kitchen and this is what I found (yikes)
  • Our spectacular energy failure: governments hobbled the market and now they must rescue it
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: European Commission backs Kyiv’s E.U. ambitions as Putin lashes out at West
  • With scant options in Ukraine, U.S. and allies prepare for long war
  • U.S. Army veteran missing in Ukraine is alive, family says, citing video
  • China unveils cutting-edge aircraft carrier, first to be locally designed
  • Russia’s gas cuts to Europe in summer could bring a bitter winter
  • Dom Phillips, journalist who chronicled Amazon deforestation, is dead at 57
  • Heat wave breaks records in France, kills baby birds in Spain
  • U.N. turns to crowdfunding to salvage oil tanker decaying off Yemen
  • Jean-Louis Trintignant, French actor of powerful reserve, dies at 91
  • Indian military’s ‘path of fire’ recruitment plan sparks mass unrest
  • Ukraine should get E.U. candidate status, European Commission recommends
  • Catholic bishop, under fire for abuse record, declines cardinal honor
  • Top British official orders Julian Assange’s extradition to U.S.
  • Mexico City trades colorful food stalls for drab uniformity
  • As nuclear test looms, North Korea reports intestinal epidemic
  • U.S. Marine veteran fighting in Ukraine has been missing since April
  • Colombia’s historic presidential election: What you need to know
  • The troubled paradox of U.S. democracy
  • Gaza rocket into Israel breaks 2-month lull
  • 2 dead in Mexico after Netflix series cast, crew van crashes
  • UNICEF: Spike in child migrants crossing the Darien Gap
  • Brazil police: Remains found those of British journalist
  • Russia frees captive medic who filmed Mariupol's horror
  • Congo's president says Rwandans waging 'economic war'
  • UN names deputy rep for Afghanistan; no word on a top envoy
  • Hawaii just expanded gender-affirming care for trans residents
  • He ran out of gas in Death Valley. Days later, he was dead, park says.
  • As Montana reeled from floods, Gov. Gianforte was nowhere to be found
  • White men charged with smashing Black teen’s car with rock, traffic cone
  • Pride events targeted in surge of anti-LGBTQ threats, violence
  • These kids don’t have fathers. He takes them fishing every weekend.
  • Tears for the racist torment my father endured
  • Video shows inmates intervene after guard was choked with a pillowcase
  • A county approved Pride Month. In June, officials overturned the vote.
  • Children served floor sealant instead of milk at Alaska day care
  • Post Politics Now: Trump attacks Jan. 6 committee, Pence and Barr in speech to conservative group
  • Cowboys for Trump founder Couy Griffin sentenced for Jan. 6 trespassing
  • Former Trump trade adviser Peter Navarro pleads not guilty to contempt
  • Biden’s ‘energy whisperer’ balances climate agenda, rising gas prices
  • Stocks stem losses but still close out worst week since March 2020
  • SpaceX fires workers who criticized Elon Musk in open letter
  • Juneteenth ice cream and paper plates: Companies keep getting holiday wrong
  • Lego prepared for questions on Youngkin and critical race theory in Va.
  • Vince McMahon steps aside as WWE CEO amid probe of hush-money allegations
  • Top Workplaces 2022
  • How D.C.-area employers kept workers happy amid the Great Resignation
  • Four Top Workplaces chiefs offer lessons on crisis management
  • A constant state of flux: Settling into being unsettled at work
  • The fight to destigmatize mental health care in the defense industry
  • 7 ways to lower your credit card debt after the Fed rate hike
  • Here’s how the 2022 Top Workplaces were selected
  • How to prepare for a recession
  • A reporter’s FOIA request scored details on EA Sports College Football
  • YouTube pulls Jan. 6 committee post, citing election disinformation
  • Stop Slacking me: How to set digital boundaries for chatty co-workers
  • Severna Park, Md., waterfront estate on the market for $15.9 million
  • How to Torch Your Unicorn
  • Australia’s Grid Is Being Cornered by Its Own Generators
  • ECB Plays Fast and Loose With the Law in Bond Plan
  • Caterpillar and Boeing Show Headquarters Don’t Matter
  • How to Access the UK Cost-of-Living Support Package
  • How Europe Became So Dependent on Putin for Its Gas
  • What Strikes and ‘Quits’ Say About US Labor’s Resolve
  • The gun-violence plague is evolving, dangerously
  • If Powell’s Fed tenure is a success, what would failure look like?
  • Will Trump run for president again? 12 of our columnists have the answer.
  • Mike Pence, stand-up guy
  • The soul-crushing lot of a Supreme Court liberal
  • A new ‘National Conservative’ manifesto sounds a lot like fascism
  • On Tuesday, voters will chart the course for a changing D.C.
  • Lauren Boebert is the latest conservative woman to get slimed
  • Jan. 6 was Mike Pence’s proudest day. He should own it.
  • How the gun talks expose the myth of an obstructionist Senate
  • Congress, don’t waste this chance on privacy legislation
  • White nationalists are getting bolder. Enforcement must, too.
  • The Post endorses John McCarthy for Montgomery County State’s Attorney
  • There were no heroes in the Trump administration on Jan. 6
  • Historically Black colleges should no longer have to do more with less
  • We warned Google that people might believe AI was sentient. Now it’s happening.
  • Pardon me
  • One step forward, two steps back
  • Cartoons by Matt Davies, Walt Handelsman, Michael Ramirez and Drew Sheneman
  • European leaders still can’t shake the urge to appease Russia
  • A young soldier’s death reminds Ukrainians that the war is taking their best
  • Don’t let the door hit you on the way out, NRA
  • Taking a class trip to D.C. doesn’t come with a license to behave badly
  • We must reimagine how to address D.C.’s persistent health inequities
  • Maryland’s offshore wind project is a $4 billion boondoggle
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 18 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-18 03:30AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Surviving Sars (2002-2007): Hong Kong’s first major health and political hurdles in 25 photos
  • ‘I need a room’: overseas Hong Kong students struggling to book quarantine hotels for summer return to city
  • Hong Kong vs Shenzhen: a technology tale of 2 cities, and a game of catch-up
  • Incoming Hong Kong leader John Lee finalises cabinet line-up, submits list to Beijing for consideration
  • Hong Kong Town Planning Board rejects proposal to switch 2 prime sites at former Kai Tak airport from commercial to housing use
  • Hong Kong health experts propose 5 key groups, including animal care workers, be vaccinated against monkeypox before exposure
  • Climate change: Hong Kong government must step up efforts to develop EV charging infrastructure to meet 2050 carbon-neutral goal, panel hears
  • Hong Kong Palace Museum previews opening shows featuring national treasures loaned by Beijing
  • Candidates running for Hong Kong arts body will need to get nominations from powerful Election Committee to ensure ‘national security elements’ addressed
  • Climate Change: Hong Kong well placed to act as go-between in carbon credits market as long as it adopts international standards, experts say
  • Hong Kong health expert jailed for 31 months over scheme to deceive WHO, other bodies into paying more than HK$4.25 million of grants, fees
  • Hong Kong protests: provision deeming zip ties ‘fit for unlawful purposes’ can be interpreted broadly, prosecutor argues in top court
  • Unlocking Students Full Potential
  • Coronavirus: surge in cases prompts Hong Kong to bolster testing; infections top 1,000 for 3 days straight
  • Hong Kong consumption vouchers: what you need to know about second batch of e-coupons, who is eligible and special offers available
  • Tencent’s WeChat debuts in 618 shopping festival with big bet on live-streaming e-commerce despite slowing economy
  • China’s US$1.5 trillion opportunity: private pension scheme entices Fidelity, Manulife, Invesco, Hong Kong asset managers
  • China manufacturing: fresh lay-offs in the Pearl River Delta add to worries over economic slowdown
  • BTS: US$3.5 million Proof album purchase shows V’s fans in China are undeterred by Beijing’s K-Culture crackdown
  • Elon Musk envisions remaking Twitter in WeChat’s image
  • Former Japanese politician’s appeal against drug trafficking conviction to be heard in Chinese court
  • TikTok moves US user data to Oracle servers amid concerns over China
  • How China inadvertently became the dominant power in Central Asia – and is radically reshaping the region
  • New US Senate bill proposes expansion of Taiwan military aid
  • Chinese scientists want to build a powerful telescope to find dark matter
  • Vladimir Putin dismisses ‘stupid’ Western sanctions on Russia over Ukraine war
  • Top Chinese influencer Xu Guohao fined US$16 million for tax evasion amid crackdown on live-streaming industry
  • China, US gas trade increase could help lift relations from ‘worst in history’, ex-official says
  • NetEase’s new game Diablo Immortal banned from new posts on Weibo just a week before hotly-anticipated release
  • Chinese mRNA booster fights Omicron 4 times better than inactivated Covid-19 vaccines, researchers say
  • China hit by floods, heatwaves and a tornado, with more weather misery on the way
  • China must ‘optimise’ zero-Covid policy or firms will be ‘unwilling to invest’, says ex-finance minister
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping to host virtual BRICS summit
  • Tangshan restaurant attack suspect was wanted by Chinese authorities over previous crimes
  • On Taiwan’s frontline islands ‘life goes on’ as jets scream overhead
  • Chinese PLA attack on Taiwan: at hand or too costly to consider?
  • US senators’ Taiwan bill to ‘restructure’ relations with Taipei
  • Explosion at pesticide firm in northwest China leaves at least 8 hurt and 6 missing, cause still unknown
  • China launches Fujian, PLA Navy’s 3rd aircraft carrier
  • New railway completes 2,700km loop of Taklamakan Desert in move to integrate Xinjiang with rest of China
  • As Japan court ruling nears, LGBT groups hope for new milestone in fight for equality
  • China’s Covid-19 health code system is ripe for abuse and must not outlast the pandemic
  • Why Singapore may be edging closer to scrapping Section 377A criminalising gay sex – and what would happen if it did
  • Fukushima disaster not state’s fault, Japan top court decides
  • G20 to raise US$1.5 billion for global pandemic fund, host Indonesia says
  • Philippine militants accused of beheading tourists surrender to authorities
  • Malaysian lawyers’ ‘walk for judicial independence’ gets off on the wrong foot
  • BTS has spoken out against anti-Asian racism in the West, but what about discrimination in South Korea?
  • Pacific can deal with its own security issues, China ‘offers economic opportunities’ – Samoa PM
  • Japan’s tougher cyberbullying laws prompted after Hana Kimura’s suicide sparks free speech debate
  • China-Australia relations: Canberra’s top diplomat Penny Wong visits Solomon Islands to address Beijing security concerns
  • Malaysia’s economic challenge shows how politics has become a bottleneck for growth
  • SpaceX fires workers behind letter criticising Elon Musk’s behaviour
  • Google co-founder Sergey Brin seeks divorce from second wife Nicole Shanahan after 3 years of marriage
  • Emmanuel Macron’s awkward moment with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelensky becomes internet meme
  • US musician reunited with stolen 262-year-old violin
  • Boris Johnson makes surprise Ukraine visit, says Russia taking heavy losses
  • Tesla’s Elon Musk sued by investor in ‘toxic’ workplace discrimination accusation
  • EU backs Ukraine’s membership bid as war brings huge historic shift
  • ‘Dark day for democracy’ as UK approves US extradition of WikiLeaks’ founder Julian Assange
  • Ukrainians threaten to destroy Russian bridge to Crimea ‘target number one’
  • Why Ukraine’s request to join is big test for EU
  • Vladimir Putin’s health: pivotal yet shrouded in uncertainty after reports of treatments
  • Russian spy caught seeking war crimes court internship, Netherlands says
  • WTO strikes landmark deals package after marathon talks, prompting cheers
  • Remembering Vincent Chin, decades after racist killing that was a flash point for Asian-Americans
  • HKEX considers opening offices in New York and London as it takes the battle for IPO fundraising to North America and Europe
  • LME to require weekly OTC position reporting as it seeks to address nickel chaos
  • Can a hawkish Fed balance the risks and avoid a US recession amid its aggressive rate increases?
  • China’s property crisis: Debt-laden Guangzhou R&F Properties seeks extension on 10 offshore bonds worth US$5.16 billion
  • Tesla raises Model Y prices again amid strong demand, Long Range SUVs to cost 5 per cent more
  • JD.com considers food delivery business, a market almost completely controlled by Meituan and Alibaba’s Ele.me
  • St Petersburg bourse to add Alibaba, Tencent, other Hong Kong stocks from June 20 in trial to widen access for Russian investors
  • Hey, Hong Kong – how about we stop being racist and include foreign domestic workers in the consumption voucher scheme?
  • The Chinatown Stalin made a ghost town: Millionka in Vladivostok, home for labourers who built the Russian Far East
  • The Zendaya of Indonesia, Zsazsa Utari – model, actress and dancer – looks to shake up its entertainment industry
  • Flamenco fashion icon inspires Dior cruise show in Spain with embroidery that uses threads of actual silver and gold
  • K-drama Yumi’s Cells season two: love is in the air as workplace romcom returns with Kim Go-eun and Jinyoung
  • How we can tap non-human intelligence – that of other animals, and plants – to save the world
  • Cheap flights with Kiwi.com saved me US$1,500 but was it worth it? My 41-hour journey from Asia to Europe
  • Cleansing foot pads do not remove toxins from your body, experts say – the foot is not a detoxifying organ
  • She gave away her US$3,000 dream wedding dress on Facebook – now other women are donating their dresses to brides in need
  • How Asia bar industry expert Holly Graham found her calling and why she’s now telling the stories of its people
  • The luxury life of Formula One’s George Russell: how the Mercedes driver and star of Netflix’s Formula 1: Drive to Survive spends big on luxury cars, designer suits and holidays with his girlfriend
  • Inside new billionaire Tiger Woods’ luxury life: besides golf winnings, he earns millions from endorsements with Nike and Tag Heuer, travelling in style in a Gulfstream private jet and superyacht
  • What are the One Direction members now? Harry Styles just topped the Billboard 2oo, Zayn Malik created new merch, Niall Horan and Louis Tomlinson are still singing – but Liam Payne is in hot water
  • Inside Britney Spears and Sam Asghari’s new marital mansion: the singer and her husband splashed out US$11.8 million on a Los Angeles home near her ex, Kevin Federline – take a look inside
  • Prince William and Kate Middleton’s new ‘normal family home’: Adelaide Cottage is located on royal grounds near Queen Elizabeth and the schools of Princes George and Louis, and Princess Charlotte
  • Hong Kong’s top Father’s Day meal deals, from The Peninsula, W Hong Kong and Agora – spoil dad rotten with Singaporean, Spanish, Cantonese and Japanese flavours
  • 12 best Italian restaurants in Hong Kong, from three-Michelin-starred 8 1/2 Otto e Mezzo Bombana and Ritz-Carlton’s Tosca di Angelo, to Sabatini Ristorante Italiano at the Royal Garden Hotel
  • Meet Lee Jae-wook, star of Netflix K-drama Alchemy of Souls: the 24-year-old rising actor was signed by Hyun Bin’s agency and looks up to Joaquin Phoenix’s The Joker
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: NY Times: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Amid Jan. 6 Revelations, Election Lies Still Dominate the G.O.P.
  • Jan. 6 Panel Could Start Sharing Transcripts With Justice Dept. in July
  • Trump Attacks Mike Pence for Not Rejecting Electoral Votes in 2020
  • What Watergate Highlights About the Jan. 6 Hearings
  • Wall Street Has Its Worst Week Since March 2020
  • Inflation Expected to Remain High Even as Economy Slows and Layoffs Rise
  • Tether: The Coin That Could Wreck Crypto
  • Russians Breached This City, Not With Troops, but Propaganda
  • Uvalde Officer Passed Up Shot at Gunman for Fear of Hitting Children
  • F.D.A. Authorizes Moderna and Pfizer Covid Vaccines for Youngest Children
  • Uterine Cancer Is on the Rise, Especially Among Black Women
  • Meet the Peecyclers. Their Idea to Help Farmers Is No. 1.
  • Chie Hayakawa Imagines a Japan Where the Elderly Volunteer to Die
  • Ginni Thomas Has a Lot of Explaining to Do
  • While Roe May Be Overturned, Feminists Fight Among Themselves
  • The Fed’s Newfound Aggressiveness Is Concerning
  • The Stock Market Is Plummeting. Welcome to the End of the ‘Everything Bubble.’
  • Sometimes ‘Proper’ Speech Isn’t Correct Speech
  • What Is Golf’s Problem? Sellout Culture.
  • Your Friday Evening Briefing
  • Jackie, Before Marrying Jack
  • What to Cook This Weekend
  • Semiconductor Makers Push Congress for New Funding
  • ‘Late Show’ Staffers Arrested at U.S. Capitol While Filming
  • Michigan’s Chaotic G.O.P. Primary for Governor: ‘It’s a Circus’
  • Ukraine News: Putin Stokes Anti-American Sentiment as Kyiv Steps Closer to E.U.
  • Colombia Will Soon Have Its First Black Female Vice President. Will It Be Her?
  • Extradition Order for Julian Assange Approved by Britain
  • India’s Military Recruitment Plan Fuels Anger and Protest
  • Paolo Macchiarini, Star Surgeon, Loses Court Ruling in Sweden
  • China Launches Third Aircraft Carrier in Major Milestone for Xi Jinping
  • She Spoke Out Against Vietnam’s Plans for Coal. Then She Was Arrested.
  • Greek Court Rejects Effort to Toughen Punishment After Killing of an American
  • Fuel-Starved and Stuck in Crisis, Sri Lanka Orders Work from Home
  • BTS Ponders Its Future, and South Korea’s Economy Warily Takes Note
  • Congo Closes Its Rwanda Border After Soldier Is Killed
  • British Journalist Dom Phillips’s Remains Identified, Brazil’s Police Say
  • Toronto Blue Jays’ Manager By Day, Salsa Musician By Night

  • Foreign Fighters in Ukraine Face Perils if Captured
  • Internal challenges like corruption pose challenges in Ukraine’s bid to join the E.U.
  • Russian Spy Stopped From Infiltrating the ICC, Dutch Intelligence Says
  • Here is what to know about the Covid vaccine for infants and toddlers.
  • Boris Johnson Pays a Second Surprise Visit to Kyiv
  • Egypt, America’s Middle Eastern ally, maintains warm relations with Putin.
  • A Better Way to Measure Immunity in Children
  • Ukraine Is Ruled Out of Staging Eurovision in 2023.
  • ‘He Took Jan. 6 Personally’
  • Yellowstone Towns Had Big Summer Plans Until Floods Struck
  • The Biggest Surprises of the Jan. 6 Hearings So Far
  • Police Error Led to Trump-Era Subpoena to Twitter About a Devin Nunes Critic
  • Florida Allows Doctors to Order Vaccines for Very Young Children
  • A man who threatened Colorado’s secretary of state on Instagram faces two years in prison.
  • The Iconic Morro Bay Smokestacks Are Coming Down
  • Arrest of Colorado 14-Year-Old Over a Slap Prompts Excessive-Force Suit
  • Proud Boys Led Major Breaches of Capitol on Jan. 6, Video Investigation Finds
  • How the Proud Boys Breached the Capitol on Jan. 6: Rile Up the Normies
  • Infighting Overshadows Big Plans at The Washington Post
  • Gun Control Advocates Have More Money Now, but Money Can’t Buy Zeal
  • Ex-Amazon Worker Convicted in Capital One Hacking
  • SpaceX Said to Fire Employees Involved in Letter Rebuking Elon Musk
  • Bear Markets and Recessions Happen More Often Than You Think
  • Explosions and Fire Break Out at Sinopec’s Shanghai Headquarters
  • Starbucks Executive, Prominent in Push Against Union Drive, Will Leave
  • TikTok says its American traffic is going through Oracle servers, but it retains backups.
  • W.T.O. Agrees to Limited Relaxing of Patent Protections on Covid Vaccines
  • How Founders Decide What They Should Get Paid
  • Covid Vaccine and Fisheries Deals Close a ‘Roller Coaster’ W.T.O. Meeting

  • Tracking the Fear Index on Wall Street

  • Dozens Injured by Hot Coals at Company Event in Zurich
  • As Some Office Workers Return, Happy Hour Sees a Wobbly Comeback

entry.title: Guardian: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Queensland cafe manager admits ‘little joke’ over Kardashian has turned into an ‘unbelievable mess’
  • Literary experts find John Hughes’ plagiarism defence unconvincing
  • Gilmore triumphs at El Salvador Pro, breaking own record for most WSL wins
  • TikTok moves to ease fears amid report workers in China accessed US users’ data
  • McIlroy sits one off Morikawa and Dahmen as battle of Brookline commences
  • Rafael Nadal hopes to make Wimbledon but reveals ‘strange’ nerve sensations
  • ‘It gives us great hope’: mom of missing US serviceman says video is authentic
  • ‘A one-sided witch-hunt’: angry Trump lashes out at January 6 hearings
  • Police say remains found in Brazilian Amazon belong to Dom Phillips
  • The Rebel Wilson uproar shows that gossip columns belong in a bygone era | Sisonke Msimang
  • What happened in the Russia-Ukraine war this week? Catch up with the must-read news and analysis
  • Bad press: the Rebel Wilson debacle that rocked SMH to its core
  • ‘Here we are’: the Iranian photographer reframing refugees
  • ‘All the worst things you can think of’: Christchurch residents stricken by city’s stench
  • SpaceX employees fired after writing letter criticizing Elon Musk
  • For safety’s sake Champions League final chaos still needs a full investigation | David Conn
  • ‘Not a place to relax’: Ynyshir, the £350-a-head Welsh techno inn atop the National Restaurant Awards
  • Julian Assange’s extradition from UK to US approved by home secretary
  • Monkey shot dead as Mexican cartels’ passion for exotic pets leaves bloody toll
  • Jos Buttler unleashes mayhem against Dutch as England smash ODI record
  • Gymnasts to demand action from governing bodies after abuse scandals
  • Geidt doubles down on claims No 10 wanted to break international law
  • Sadio Mané leaving Liverpool after £35m Bayern Munich deal accepted
  • How the final journey of Dom Phillips and Bruno Pereira ended in tragedy
  • UN calls on UAE to release British man imprisoned in Dubai since 2008
  • Putin calls Ukraine war sanctions ‘insane’ in combative speech
  • Biden says Americans are ‘really, really down’ in rare one-on-one interview
  • France and Turkey propose rival plans to get grain out of Ukraine
  • The Guardian view on Julian Assange’s extradition: a bad day for journalism | Editorial
  • Cleaner who led pay strike faces former minister in Paris election
  • ‘Farmgate’ threatens Cyril Ramaphosa’s South Africa re-election bid
  • Seth Meyers: Ginni Thomas’ texts ‘make Mike Lindell sound like a levelheaded businessman’
  • Fight against inflation raises spectre of global recession
  • Canadian priest arrested for 1960s sexual assault at First Nations residential school
  • Boris Johnson promises Ukraine UK-led troop training scheme on Kyiv visit
  • Ukraine deserves its place in the EU. It’s right for the country – and right for Europe | Timothy Garton Ash
  • Jean-Louis Trintignant: an actor of charisma, depth and dark emotions | Peter Bradshaw
  • Britain has a decision to make: the rule of Boris Johnson – or the rule of law? | Jonathan Freedland
  • The week Boris Johnson went to war with the law
  • Ex-BP executive lands $6.2m payout at US firm despite death of offshore worker

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
